cohesion_notes: Aguirre has an experienced core, but notable omissions and goalkeeper/injury churn disrupted the final buildup.
quality_notes: Balanced unit: Jimenez, Gimenez, Quinones and Vega offer varied attacking options; Montes, Vasquez and Alvarez provide a solid but not elite defensive spine.
dropout_notes: Lozano and Henry Martin omitted; Malagon, Huescas, Orozco and Ruiz missed the selected squad through injury, reducing depth.
injury_notes: Gimenez recently battled an ankle issue but made the squad; Alvarez, Chavez and Vega are available but need management.

quality_notes: Son, Lee Kang-in and Hwang give the selected squad above-average pace, creation and finishing; the defensive group is experienced but less distinguished.

cohesion_notes: Miroslav Koubek only took over in Dec. 2025, leaving limited World Cup prep time.
quality_notes: Schick, Hlozek and Sulc give Czechia a strong central attacking core; Kovar, Holes and Krejci make the defence broadly tournament-average.
dropout_notes: David Zima and Lukas Provod missed out injured.
injury_notes: Jan Kuchta carried an ankle issue into camp.

quality_notes: Foster and the domestic-based forwards offer transition threat but limited top-level scoring depth; Williams and the Sundowns-Orlando core form a coherent average defence.
Embolo and the supporting forwards are capable without being prolific; Akanji, Kobel and an experienced European-based back line are a clear squad strength.
injury_notes: Amdouni only recently returned from ACL rehab and arrived with very limited club minutes. Embolo travel approval was delayed but has reportedly been resolved.

cohesion_notes: Several starters are returning from layoffs, leaving Marsch with unsettled early-tournament combinations.
quality_notes: David, Davies and rapid wide runners give Canada an above-average transition attack; the back line has athleticism but less proven depth than the forward unit.
dropout_notes: Marcelo Flores tore his ACL and missed the final squad.
injury_notes: Davies is a doubt for the opener only; David, Bombito and Ahmed are back and in the squad.

age_notes: Edin Dzeko is 40 and still the focal point for goals and leadership.
quality_notes: Dzeko, Demirovic and Dedic provide credible attacking quality, while the defensive personnel are solid but lack elite depth.
injury_notes: Dzeko doubtful opener; Tabakovic muscle injury; fitness doubts

age_notes: Sebastian Soria is 42, with Al-Haydos and Boudiaf also 35 in an older core.
cohesion_notes: Lopetegui is still bedding in a veteran-heavy core after a coaching change this cycle.
quality_notes: Afif and Almoez Ali remain a proven creative and finishing partnership; the defensive unit is experienced regionally but vulnerable against higher-level pace and pressure.

cohesion_notes: Ouahbi replaced Regragui in March 2026, then made major selection calls and moved on from several 2022 leaders, creating a real short-prep cohesion risk.
quality_notes: Brahim Diaz, El Khannouss and direct wide options give Morocco high-end creation; Hakimi, Mazraoui and Aguerd anchor an above-average defensive unit.
dropout_notes: Ziyech, En-Nesyri, Boufal, Adli and Ben Seghir were coach choices; replacements like Brahim Diaz are in-form.
injury_notes: Aguerd made the squad after a long injury layoff and was not fully sharp.

cohesion_notes: Ancelotti is new to the role and Neymar is being reintegrated with managed fitness/media expectations, creating mild tactical and dressing-room uncertainty.
quality_notes: Vinicius, Raphinha, Neymar and Endrick give Brazil elite one-on-one creation and finishing variety; Marquinhos leads a strong defence despite important absences.
dropout_notes: Rodrygo, Estevao and Eder Militao missed the selected squad through injury, a major multi-starter/talent hit despite Brazil's depth.
injury_notes: Neymar made the squad but is managing a calf issue and may not be fully ready for the opener.

age_notes: Average age is around 29; Gordon is 43 and several key outfielders are 31-34.
cohesion_notes: Late injuries forced changes to Clarke's midfield/forward plans and reduced continuity.
quality_notes: McTominay supplies midfield goals but the selected forwards lack consistent elite finishing; Robertson, Tierney and the central defenders make the defensive unit competitive.
dropout_notes: Billy Gilmour (knee) and Tommy Conway withdrew from the finals squad.
injury_notes: Che Adams has a thigh issue, while Gordon and Hanley only recently returned.

quality_notes: Nazon, Pierrot and Bellegarde offer isolated threat, but the squad has very limited top-flight creation, finishing and defensive depth compared with the tournament field.
Yildiz, Guler and Calhanoglu provide high-level creativity and shooting; the defensive personnel are capable but less convincing than the attacking group.
injury_notes: Kenan Yildiz is doubtful for the opener with a calf injury; Akturkoglu only just returned.

age_notes: This is the fifth-youngest US World Cup squad ever, with 13 of 26 at their first finals.
cohesion_notes: Pochettino is at his first major tournament in charge, and the midfield depth chart is still unsettled.
quality_notes: Pulisic, Balogun and a mobile supporting cast give the hosts above-average attacking talent; the defence is athletic but has unsettled positions and goalkeeper questions.
dropout_notes: Tessmann missed out with a muscle injury; Luna was omitted after an injury-hit buildup.
injury_notes: Chris Richards is a doubt for the opener after a late-season knock.

quality_notes: Almiron, Enciso and Sanabria make Paraguay dangerous in transition without sustained elite creation; the selected back line and midfield screen are strong and physical.
dropout_notes: Mathias Villasanti missed the tournament injured.

cohesion_notes: Seventeen World Cup debutants and late integration of Volpato make this a new-look group.
quality_notes: Australia have useful direct runners and aerial threat but limited top-level creation; Souttar and an experienced structure keep the defence around tournament average.
dropout_notes: Riley McGree was ruled out by a hamstring injury.

quality_notes: Musiala, Wirtz and Havertz provide elite creation although the selected forward depth is reduced; Rudiger, Tah and strong goalkeeping make the defence above average.
dropout_notes: Gnabry is out, and Jonathan Burkardt also missed out with a calf injury.

quality_notes: Ecuador have pace and transition ability but no consistently elite finisher; Hincapie, Pacho, Caicedo and a powerful athletic structure make chance prevention a major strength.
dropout_notes: Arboleda, Byron Castillo, Dominguez and Cifuentes were notable omissions.

cohesion_notes: Emerse Fae is integrating several new forwards and returnees from many different leagues.
quality_notes: Amad, Pepe and Yan Diomande offer pace and invention; Ndicka, Singo and Ousmane Diomande form one of the tournament's strongest non-elite defensive groups.
dropout_notes: Haller, Zaha and injured defender Clement Akpa headline several big omissions.
injury_notes: Guessand's match rhythm is uncertain after injury, though Kessie recovered from his scare.

cohesion_notes: Advocaat briefly stepped away before returning, creating a late coaching wobble ahead of Curaçao's first finals.
quality_notes: Chong and Bacuna add technical quality, but the squad lacks reliable high-level scoring and has limited defensive depth against World Cup opposition.

cohesion_notes: Koeman had fitness checks and some selection debate, but the final squad still has strong defensive depth.
quality_notes: Depay, Gakpo and Simons provide varied attacking quality; Van Dijk, Timber and a deep group of top-level defenders give the Netherlands an elite defensive ceiling.
dropout_notes: Xavi Simons and Jerdy Schouten missed the selected squad with ACL injuries; de Ligt/de Vrij fitness omissions and Frimpong coach omission hurt, but depth prevents a critical hit.
injury_notes: Timber, Depay and other recent returnees need monitoring, though most core starters are available.

quality_notes: Kubo and the remaining European-based attackers retain technical quality but key attacking absences reduce the unit; Tomiyasu and a disciplined structure support an above-average defence.
dropout_notes: Mitoma, Minamino and Morita missed the selected squad through injury, taking away major attacking/midfield quality.
injury_notes: Tomiyasu returned after injury absence and needs management; other core players have recent fitness load concerns.

cohesion_notes: Graham Potter took over mid-qualification and is still bedding in new combinations.
quality_notes: Isak and Gyokeres give Sweden two outstanding finishers even without Kulusevski; the defensive group is experienced but closer to tournament average.
dropout_notes: Kulusevski is out after knee surgery; Bardghji and Swedberg were left out.
injury_notes: Gudmundsson's recent hamstring injury clouds his opener fitness.

cohesion_notes: Lamouchi has made sweeping changes from AFCON 2025, leaving a less-settled group.
quality_notes: Tunisia have industrious forwards but limited high-end creation and finishing; the selected defensive unit is organised and broadly tournament-average.
dropout_notes: Retired/not-selected veterans Sliti, Sassi and Meriah headline the generational change.

age_notes: Courtois, De Bruyne, Lukaku, Meunier and Witsel are all 33+, so the core is aging.
cohesion_notes: Garcia is balancing fading Golden Generation leaders with several newer faces.
quality_notes: De Bruyne, Doku and Lukaku still provide top-level creation and finishing; Courtois and experienced defenders keep the defensive unit above average despite its age profile.
injury_notes: Debast has a hamstring issue, while Lukaku and De Ketelaere carried recent fitness concerns.

age_notes: Average age is around 29; Salah turns 34 and both senior goalkeepers are 37-38.
cohesion_notes: Selection shocks and five debutants leave some uncertainty around chemistry.
quality_notes: Salah and Marmoush give Egypt elite individual counterattacking threat; the rest of the defensive unit is organised but not exceptional by tournament standards.
dropout_notes: Mostafa Mohamed and El-Shenawy were coach omissions; Fathi injured but depth covers the position.
injury_notes: Abdelmonem is in the squad after a long injury absence and may lack sharpness.

quality_notes: Wood is a proven focal point but creation behind him is limited; the defensive pool lacks depth and top-level pace across the full tournament squad.

age_notes: One of the oldest squads in the field, with many starters 32+ and average age near 30.
cohesion_notes: War stopped the domestic league, 17 players lack recent matches, and Iran had to change base late.
quality_notes: Taremi remains a high-level forward but Azmoun's omission and limited supporting depth reduce the attack; the experienced defensive structure remains competitive.
dropout_notes: Sardar Azmoun remained omitted after a political/federation fallout.

depth_notes: Multiple top-level options in midfield, wide attack and defence support rotation without materially weakening the team.
quality_notes: Yamal, Nico Williams, Olmo and a technically dominant midfield create an elite attack; Raya, Rodri and a deep press-resistant defensive group provide elite prevention and control.
dropout_notes: Fermin Lopez missed the selected squad with a broken foot; Morata, Carvajal and Huijsen were coach omissions in a no-Madrid-player squad.
injury_notes: Yamal, Nico Williams and Merino all arrived after recent injury scares or layoffs.

cohesion_notes: Bielsa's generational shift and the omission of veteran leaders remain a major talking point.
quality_notes: Nunez, Valverde and strong wide runners give Uruguay dangerous vertical attacking quality; Araujo, Gimenez and Ugarte support an aggressive above-average defence.
dropout_notes: Suarez and Nandez were omitted, while Piquerez, De Arrascaeta, Pellistri and Vina had injuries around selection.
injury_notes: Rochet and Caceres are managing recent injuries in camp.

cohesion_notes: Georgios Donis only arrived weeks before the tournament and lacks a natural left-back in the final squad.
quality_notes: The selected forwards have movement and regional experience but limited elite finishing; the defence lacks depth and top-level individual quality against this field.
dropout_notes: Hawsawi and Abu Al-Shamat were left out for fitness reasons; Al-Salem also missed the cut.

age_notes: Ryan Mendes is 36, Vozinha 39, and several other starters are 35+, so the core is old for a debutant.
cohesion_notes: First World Cup and a diaspora-heavy squad mean chemistry will be tested on a new stage.
quality_notes: Ryan Mendes and a physical forward group can threaten directly but creation is limited; the experienced, athletic defensive unit is competitive enough to rate around average.
injury_notes: Logan Costa only just returned from ACL rehab and is being managed carefully.

depth_notes: Elite replacements across attack, midfield, defence and goalkeeper allow meaningful rotation with little quality loss.
quality_notes: Mbappe and a deep group of elite forwards give France exceptional creation and finishing; Maignan, Saliba, Konate and the wider defensive pool are elite across every role.
dropout_notes: Ekitike missed the selected squad with a torn Achilles; Kolo Muani and Camavinga were coach omissions from a deep squad.
injury_notes: Kamara is a late doubt, while Mbappe and several others only recently shook off minor issues.

quality_notes: Haaland, Odegaard and Sorloth form one of the tournament's most productive and complementary attacks; the defensive personnel are functional rather than elite.
Mane, Jackson and Sarr provide pace, power and varied finishing; Mendy, Koulibaly and a strong athletic core keep the defence above tournament average.
Iraq have mobile forwards and counterattacking potential but limited proven top-level finishing; the defensive unit is disciplined and broadly balanced for the field.

age_notes: Average age is about 29; Messi turns 39 during the tournament and Otamendi is 38.
depth_notes: Proven tournament-quality alternatives across the forward line, midfield and central defence provide exceptional replacement depth.
quality_notes: Messi, Alvarez and Lautaro lead an elite, highly complementary attack; Martinez, Romero and Lisandro anchor a strong defence, though parts of the unit are aging.
dropout_notes: Acuna, Senesi and Buendia were notable omissions as Scaloni refreshed around the old core.
injury_notes: Messi, Emi Martinez, Romero, Molina, Montiel and Nico Gonzalez all had recent fitness issues but are all in the squad.

quality_notes: Arnautovic and midfield runners provide a coherent but not elite attack; Alaba, Danso and Rangnick's pressing structure make the defensive unit above average.

cohesion_notes: Injuries forced a defensive and goalkeeping reshuffle, reducing continuity.
quality_notes: Mahrez, Amoura, Gouiri and Hadj Moussa give Algeria pace, technique and multiple routes to goal; the defence is experienced but less deep than the attack.
dropout_notes: Mandrea missed the selected squad injured, while Bennacer and Atal also missed the final cut.
injury_notes: Goalkeeper fitness is shaky, and Luca Zidane, Mastil, Titraoui and Gouiri all had recent issues.

cohesion_notes: First World Cup and a younger reworked attacking core make Jordan less settled than in qualifying.
quality_notes: Al-Tamari supplies genuine transition quality but the selected attack lacks depth around him; a compact, disciplined defensive unit rates around tournament average.
dropout_notes: Al-Naimat, Al-Quraishi, Smeiri and Bani Odeh missed the selected squad through injury, removing key attacking options.

depth_notes: Top-club alternatives cover nearly every position, including several different attacking and defensive profiles.
quality_notes: Bruno Fernandes, Leao, Bernardo and a deep forward group provide elite creation around Ronaldo; Dias, Mendes and a deep set of top-club defenders form an elite defence.
dropout_notes: Palhinha, Antonio Silva and Mateus Fernandes were surprise omissions.

age_notes: Half the squad is 30+; James, Ospina and Vargas are all in their mid-30s.
cohesion_notes: Duran's exclusion followed a clear discipline rift, even if the rest of camp looks stable.
quality_notes: Luis Diaz, James and quick supporting forwards create a dangerous counterattacking unit; Lucumi, Munoz and a physical midfield give Colombia an above-average defence.
dropout_notes: Duran discipline drop; Borre, Cuadrado and Villa were coach choices with adequate replacements.

cohesion_notes: Cannavaro only arrived in late 2025, and every player is a World Cup debutant.
quality_notes: Shomurodov and Fayzullaev provide credible attacking quality, while Khusanov raises the defensive ceiling; overall depth remains around tournament average.
dropout_notes: Ibrokhimkhalil Yuldoshev misses the finals injured.

cohesion_notes: A late replacement and several newer dual-national additions reduce continuity a little.
quality_notes: Wissa and Bakambu give DR Congo direct attacking threat without elite creative depth; Mbemba and a powerful, athletic defensive group are a clear strength.
dropout_notes: Rocky Bushiri withdrew injured and was replaced by Aaron Tshibola.

cohesion_notes: Tuchel is at his first major tournament in charge and is blending several debutants with a deep squad.
depth_notes: Despite notable omissions, the selected squad still has high-level alternatives across attack, midfield and the full-back positions.
quality_notes: Kane, Saka and Bellingham still form an elite attacking core despite major omissions; Stones, Guehi, Rice and strong full-back options keep the defence above average.
dropout_notes: Several high-profile players were omitted, including Palmer, Foden, Alexander-Arnold, Grealish and Maguire, but England's depth softens the hit.
injury_notes: Stones, Reece James and other recent fitness-management cases keep a mild availability risk.

age_notes: Modric is 40, Perisic 37, and much of Croatia's spine is now 32+.
quality_notes: Kramaric and the selected forwards are technically sound but lack elite pace and volume; Gvardiol, Sutalo and Livakovic support an above-average defence.
dropout_notes: Petkovic and Majer missed the final squad after injury-hit seasons.
injury_notes: Gvardiol, Modric and Kovacic are only just back from recent injuries.

age_notes: Average age is about 30, with Quintero 38, Godoy 36 and several other starters 35+.
quality_notes: Panama have physical runners and set-piece threat but limited top-level creation and finishing; their experienced defensive structure is competitive rather than weak.
injury_notes: Carrasquilla is in the squad but dealing with a groin injury.

cohesion_notes: Queiroz is blending returnees and inexperienced options to cover structural gaps left by injuries.
quality_notes: Without Kudus the selected attack lacks a proven elite creator, while the absences of Salisu and Djiku leave the defensive unit short of top-level quality and depth.
dropout_notes: Kudus, Salisu and Djiku missed the selected squad through injury, stripping quality across attack and defence.
injury_notes: Nuamah is back after ACL recovery and Rahman returns after a long national-team absence.
